title: Bike Touring - Germany - Showing all my Gear for my European Bike Tour 2010

transcript.text: Hello, I figured I'd do a video of all my gear. I did a video of the gear I wanted to bring,
But I think I'll do another video of the gear that I actually am bringing.
So, I've got the video camera, the little handheld video camera that I'm filming myself with,
And the tripod is holding it up, and then I have the remote, and what else do I have?
I've got the clothes I have now. I'm going to include the bike shoes. I have special bike socks.
These bike socks are cool because they keep the water rolls off of them.
Then I have the leggings, then I have the bike shorts, and then I have this jacket,
Which I should have slept in last night because I was freezing, so I'll definitely make sure to sleep in tonight.
I've got this spandex shirt, and under this spandex shirt, I've got the jersey,
And then underneath the jacket, I've got the armies.
And if it's raining outside, I have the rain jacket, and then I have the rain pants.
And I also have a pair of, another pair of Viking socks.
I have two pairs of socks. I use these socks at night.
I fill it up with water, and it holds water quite well because it's like waterproof.
And I squish it up like this, and I get it totally wet.
And then I wipe my neck here so that when I sleep, I don't have to feel the sticky skin.
And that's pretty much the only place I have to wash at night.
Last night, I did do my arms here and my leg pits, but it's really not necessary because I sleep in my tights,
So it doesn't really matter.
And when I do, and I also have my Viking shorts on, which I showed you,
But when I sleep, I take the shorts off and I put these shorts on.
Although maybe I shouldn't have done that last night because it was kind of cold.
And so when I walk around town, Mama says I should try and fit in, and she's right.
But it's good to fit in, so I've got these shorts, and I've got this t-shirt.
So, walking around, normal, looking normal.
See those? But those are the, those are, and then I have the hat that I've got on.
And that's all the clothes that I have.
I also have a watch.
I have my little balance thing.
I have my Lance Armstrong bracelet.
And that's all that I have.
Okay, now as far as the bike, I have two pumps.
So I have a pump on the bike, which I bought yesterday
Because I left my other pump at the, in the bathroom a couple days ago.
I have four water bottles.
I did send my Camelback back.
That was overdoing it.
I don't have that much space.
So, let's see what else I have here.
Okay, so when it gets really cold, well, I do have my,
Where are they?
I, um, I can't see them here, but I do have my, um,
A normal pair of, uh, here we are.
I've got a nice, thick pair of biking, um, gloves.
And my helmet.
I have, also, if it gets cold, I have long finger gloves I can put on.
I don't put them on over the bike gloves.
I just take the bike gloves off, put those on, because those are bike gloves, too.
And I have booties.
So, if it's really cold, I can have the bike gloves on.
The long fingered ones, I can have two pairs of socks on,
And these booties over the shoes.
I did wear the booties one day, because it was cold, and they kept me nice and warm.
Um, now I, I also, I brought a sleeping bag,
But I brought a new sleeping bag while I was out here, because it was too cold.
Plus, this one packs smaller.
Um, this is the stuff stack for it, and it packs like that big.
It packs like that big.
It's really tiny.
But I have, also, another bag to put it inside of, that's a waterproof, in case it rains.
And I have, um, plastic bags to put all my electronic gear in, like my video camera.
And this is my cell phone in a plastic bag, in case it rains.
Um, and I keep my cell phone turned off, because I have international phone,
And if anybody calls me while I'm riding my bike or something, um, I, I'm paid.
I have to pay for when they're ringing, and when they're leaving the message and stuff.
But I don't have to pay if the phone's turned off when they leave the message and stuff, so.
Um, but, I just call my mom every few days.
It's a minute, it's a dollar a minute, so.
Um, now, so.
I have a computer.
And I'll show you how I have my computer set up.
This is my backpack that I strap on the bike with this strap, and this strap.
And I also have this red strap on the back, which I think helps cinch it down a little bit, too.
And the bike rack right now is situated on my, um, my seat post.
Um, which has been working okay.
I kind of destroyed my frame.
I hope it hangs in there.
But I have a bike lock, which I bought when I was in Amsterdam, because I forgot to bring
My lock, but this is a good lock.
Um, it just hangs right in there, and it can lock up my frame and the two wheels.
Now, and this bag has two major compartments.
There's this big, big one here, which I put on my clothes.
And then this one on top fits my, my computer perfectly.
Um, now when I put my computer in, I do have my computer in its special little bag.
But when I can put my computer in, I make sure that this, that the, that the top part
Is down facing the sleep, the backpack.
Because I was stupid, and I had it up before, and when I cinched it, the backpack down really
Hard on my bike, I cracked my screen.
So now my screen has splotches all over it and cracks across it.
But since I've been cinching it down with the computer part up, it pushes the screen against
The sleeping, the backpack, and it hasn't cracked my screen anymore.
So, I think all the, like, bumps and stuff, the vibration of writing, the computer is protected
Cushioned by all the clothing below it.
And it's not getting cracked when it gets cinched.
So, hopefully it doesn't get any worse.
But I, I do not have a, a plastic bag for my sleeping, my computer yet for when it rains.
So I have to remember to get one of those.
It's kind of hard to get those in Europe because they don't have, uh, plastic bags in the supermarkets.
Which is cool.
Now, I also have an extra battery for my computer.
Um, these little mini computers are cool because the batteries last longer than normal computers.
And I wouldn't want to bring a normal computer anyways because it's so big.
I have an extra axle because I did break my axle, like, on the second day.
Um, so I have an extra axle in case I break an axle again.
Maybe those plastic, those carbon fiber wheels are a little bit harder on the axle than the
Normal wheels, perhaps.
Because they, uh, maybe they're more, they're harder, more rigid.
I have an extra bag with my passport and my, uh, I think traveler's insurance or my international
Driver's license which my mom made me get.
So she paid for it.
I was like, you make me get it, you pay for it.
Actually, she offered it.
Because I'm never going to use it.
But she says it's good to have for an extra way of identification or something like that.
Okay.
Now, I also have, this is my front bag.
So I don't really have that much in it.
Um, I have my computer here on the top.
I have my headlamp for when I'm, when light on my tent or when I'm riding down the road,
I can just strap it on my helmet and it doesn't fall off.
It's pretty cool.
And, um, well, these are the two chargers for my cell phone and my, um, and my, uh, MP3 player.
I have a special charger for my MP3 player.
I mean, my video camera.
My cell phone and my video camera.
I don't have one for my video MP3 player because the MP3 player takes the one, the same one as my cell phone.
But I don't charge it that way anyway.
I charge it with my computer.
That's like the main reason I like having a computer.
If my screen does goes completely out, I'm still going to want to keep my computer because it charges my two MP3 players.
I have a, a calm.
Here's one MP3 player number one.
Here's my earphones and MP3 player number two.
I wish I had three MP3 players, but I couldn't find one when I left.
And, um, I do have an extra, like, brake and derailleur cable.
I think that's overkill, though.
I'll probably throw that away.
Um, I do have an extra little lock.
I don't think I need that, but here's a patch kit.
Um, I got an inhaler.
I should get a new one, though.
Um, because I've got asthma every night and this one's maybe going out.
Here's my extra pump.
I do have two pumps.
I do have an extra battery for my video camera, too.
Here's my tools, my little Allen tools.
I have a, uh, a toothbrush that's magnetic, so I don't need toothpaste, so that saves me a little space.
Got a bunch of these, uh, uh, tire irons.
I have two extra tubes here.
Um, I think I have three extra tubes.
Yeah, that one and these two.
These two are patchless, which is good.
Here's another, um, patch kit.
I do have a patch kit in here with all my extra, um, cards, memory cards.
I have a, um, Swiss Army knife.
I did have a, um, Leatherman, but it, the guys tore the freaking box open and stole my Leatherman on the airport.
I have a spoon and a fork for if I want to get, um, make my own food, you know, make my own salad or buy yogurt or something.
I have thread.
I've got athlete's foot cream.
This is a special opener and closer for the tire.
Sometimes it's really cold out and the tire, you know, doesn't want to stretch over the frame,
So this thing pulls it over.
So it's pretty cool.
So that's basically all I have in here.
And then I use the extra space in here to, like, hold my leggings and armings and stuff
For when I take them off and I'm riding and I don't want to unstrap my, my backpack.
Oh my god, I'm out of time.
Okay.
And then my tent and my, my, my, my, my, uh, toiletry stuff and my, my map.
And that's about it.
Okay.
